sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.98 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$$be a saturated formation, and$G$a finite soluble minimal non-$$-group such that$G^{ }$is a Sylow$p$-sub\-group of$G$. Is it true that$G^{ }$is isomorphic to a factor group of the Sylow$p$-sub\-group of$PSU(3, p^{2n})$? This is true for the formation of finite nilpotent groups (V.\,D.\,Mazurov, S.\,A.\,Syskin, Math. Notes, 14, no.\,2 (1973), 683--686; \ A.\,Kh.\,Zhurtov, S.\,A.\,Syskin, Siberi...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
